problem:
Compute the distance between the parallel lines given by
\[\begin{pmatrix} 2 \\ -3 \end{pmatrix} + t \begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ -7 \end{pmatrix}\]and
\[\begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ -5 \end{pmatrix} + s \begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ -7 \end{pmatrix}.\]

classified_questions.question: What is the definition of the distance between two parallel lines in $\mathbb{R}^2$?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we use vectors to find the distance between two parallel lines?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: In the given problem, what are the equations of the two parallel lines in vector form?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the vector $\bold{v}$ that joins a point on one line to a point on the other line?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How do we find the projection $\bold{p}$ of $\bold{v}$ onto the direction vector $\bold{d}$ of the lines?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the formula for the distance between two parallel lines using the projection?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the vector $\bold{c}$ that connects a point on one line to the point on the other line that is closest to it?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How do we use $\bold{c}$ to find the distance between the two parallel lines in this problem?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can we use a similar method, involving projections and orthogonal vectors, to find the distance between two non-parallel lines in $\mathbb{R}^3$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: How does the distance between two parallel lines change when one of the lines is shifted?
classified_questions.label: b
